message:
style: apply rustfmt across all connectors and infrastructure crates

Mechanical formatting pass across 184 files: alphabetize imports
(fcp_async_core before fcp_core before thiserror), collapse single-line
ConnectorRuntimeConfig chains, remove spurious blank lines between
import groups, vertically expand long assert!/matches! macro calls,
and reformat chained method calls to satisfy line-length limits.

Also:
- .rchignore: add **/.beads/ and **/.br_recovery/ patterns
- Minor formatting in fcp-host, fcp-oauth, fcp-testkit, fwc crates

No behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.4","title":"Mattermost: websocket event stream, threads, channels, and files","description":"Implement the websocket or event-stream handling, thread or channel state mapping, file upload or download paths, reconnect policy, and inbound event translation required for Mattermost to behave as a first-class real-time collaboration connector.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:58:16.861640Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:12:47.253414Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:12:47.252712Z","close_reason":"Implemented websocket streaming, thread/channel/file support, and verified fcp-mattermost via rch clippy/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:58:16.861640Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:58:16.861640Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","title":"[T1-MSG.7] fcp.nextcloud-talk — Nextcloud Talk Collaboration Connector","description":"# Nextcloud Talk Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Nextcloud Talk is the most broadly useful self-hosted SMB collaboration connector missing from the campaign. It complements hosted messaging tools while pulling FCP closer to privacy-conscious and self-managed deployments.

## API surface
- Nextcloud OCS / Talk APIs for rooms, messages, participants, mentions, attachments, and room metadata
- Webhook or event-driven inbound notifications where available, with polling fallback expectations documented explicitly
- App password or token-based authentication against a self-hosted Nextcloud instance

## Expected operations
- `nextcloud_talk.messages.send|get|list`
- `nextcloud_talk.rooms.list|get|create`
- `nextcloud_talk.participants.list|add|remove`
- `nextcloud_talk.files.upload|get`
- `nextcloud_talk.calls.get_state` where supported by the exposed API surface

## V3 considerations
- Self-hosted base URL and admin-configured app capabilities must be reflected truthfully in readiness and doctor outputs
- Must separate room-management writes from message-content writes in `OperationInfo` risk modeling
- Needs clear operator guidance around Talk app enablement, auth setup, and event-delivery expectations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","title":"Nextcloud Talk: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix","description":"Define the V3 contract for Nextcloud Talk before implementation expands. Pin down the supported Nextcloud and Talk app versions, auth model, room or message or participant or file operation inventory, event-delivery assumptions, and the boundary between Talk messaging, room administration, and broader Nextcloud functionality.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:41.682898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:30:10.274326Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:30:10.273768Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:41.682898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:41.682898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:41.682898Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1838,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for Nextcloud Talk:

**Archetype**: Bidirectional + Polling (self-hosted collaboration)
**Auth model**: Nextcloud user credentials (username + app password) or OAuth 2.0. Server URL configurable. App passwords recommended over user passwords.
**Network**: Nextcloud server URL (configurable). TLS recommended. deny_localhost=false (self-hosted), deny_private=false.

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| nextcloud_talk.send_message | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | nextcloud_talk.send |
| nextcloud_talk.delete_message | Dangerous | High | Strict | nextcloud_talk.send |
| nextcloud_talk.get_conversations | Safe | Low | None | nextcloud_talk.read |
| nextcloud_talk.get_messages | Safe | Low | None | nextcloud_talk.read |
| nextcloud_talk.get_participants | Safe | Low | None | nextcloud_talk.read |
| nextcloud_talk.send_reaction | Risky | Low | BestEffort | nextcloud_talk.send |
| nextcloud_talk.set_read_marker | Risky | Low | BestEffort | nextcloud_talk.send |
| nextcloud_talk.share_file | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | nextcloud_talk.send |
| nextcloud_talk.health | Safe | Low | Strict | nextcloud_talk.read |

**Polling**: Talk API uses polling (GET /ocs/v2.php/apps/spreed/api/v1/chat/{token}?lookIntoFuture=1). Long-poll with timeout. Cursor: lastKnownMessageId. No native WebSocket (Nextcloud-specific HPB may exist but not standard).

**Safety policy**: Message deletion is Dangerous (server removes content). File sharing uses Nextcloud Files API internally. Rate limits: server-configurable. Server version >= Talk 17.0 (Nextcloud 27+) for full API.

**Provisioning**: Create app password in Nextcloud Settings > Security. Doctor checks: server reachable, credentials valid, Talk app enabled, API version compatible.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No conversation creation/administration, no call/video signaling, no file management (beyond sharing to chat), no Nextcloud Deck/Calendar integration.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:30:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","title":"Nextcloud Talk: typed config, client, error mapping, and state model","description":"Build the self-hosted Talk foundation for base URL config, auth, room or message identifiers, participant state, attachment helpers, paging or polling cursors, and ConnectorErrorMapping-backed API error normalization.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:59:55.741325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.3","title":"Nextcloud Talk: connector runtime, manifest, operation handlers, and introspection","description":"Implement the V3 connector surface for Nextcloud Talk: ConnectorRuntime lifecycle, truthful manifest declarations about Talk app prerequisites and network constraints, typed introspection, capability scope, and request or response handling for room and message workflows.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:00:09.932208Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:00:09.932208Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:00:09.932208Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:00:09.932208Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:00:09.932208Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","title":"[T1-MSG.1.1] WhatsApp: types, client, error modules","description":"Create connectors/whatsapp/ crate scaffold with:
- src/types.rs: Message types (TextMessage, MediaMessage, TemplateMessage,
  InteractiveMessage, LocationMessage, ContactMessage), webhook payload types
  (WebhookEntry, WebhookChange, WebhookValue, IncomingMessage, MessageStatus),
  API response types (SendMessageResponse, ProfileResponse)
- src/client.rs: WhatsAppClient with ConnectorRuntime, HttpRetryConfig,
  RetryLoop::execute for all API calls, auth header injection, base URL
  configuration, rate limit handling (429 → Retryable with retry-after)
- src/error.rs: WhatsAppError enum (Http, Json, Api, RateLimited, Unauthorized,
  InvalidPhoneNumber, TemplateRejected) with ConnectorErrorMapping impl
- Cargo.toml with fcp-core, fcp-sdk, fcp-async-core, reqwest, serde, serde_json,
  thiserror, tracing, wiremock (dev), chrono deps

## Evidence required
- cargo check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean
- Error Display/Debug formats tested
- ConnectorErrorMapping roundtrip tested","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:18.241245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:37:42.553030Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:37:42.552519Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:02.861395Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:26.175977Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:40.604707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:22:26.339129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:40:16.470612Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","title":"[T1-MSG.3] fcp.signal — Signal Messenger Connector (via signal-cli bridge)","description":"# Signal Connector (via `signal-cli` bridge)

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Signal is one of the most important privacy-first messaging gaps in the current connector inventory. It does not have the raw global reach of WhatsApp, but it is disproportionately important for security-sensitive teams and users who explicitly avoid cloud-hosted chat platforms.

## API surface and operating model
This connector targets a self-hosted `signal-cli` bridge rather than a first-party bot API. That choice has architectural consequences that must stay visible in the implementation plan:
- outbound send operations are bridge-mediated request/response calls,
- inbound messages and group events are bridge-backed polling or event delivery flows,
- account registration and trust state are external prerequisites,
- readiness and doctor outputs must explain bridge health instead of pretending Signal is a plain hosted API.

## Expected operations
- `signal.messages.send_text|send_attachment`
- `signal.messages.receive|list`
- `signal.contacts.list|get`
- `signal.groups.list|get`
- `signal.identities.trust`
- `signal.profile.get`

## V3 implementation shape
This feature is intentionally broken into a five-step V3 ladder:
1. define the connector contract, auth boundary, bridge topology, safety matrix, and first-slice non-goals;
2. build typed config, client, state, and error mapping for the bridge boundary;
3. wire the V3 runtime, manifest, handlers, and introspection surfaces;
4. implement receive loops, group/message state handling, and attachment flows;
5. close with readiness, remediation guidance, privacy-safe observability, and explicit conformance evidence.

## Critical considerations
- The manifest must declare the bridge prerequisite truthfully.
- Logging and doctor output must never leak sensitive identifiers or message content.
- The first safe slice should stay conservative about identity/trust mutation semantics.
- The verification bead must prove the bridge-backed architecture is operable, not just compilable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:37:09.516287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1784,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:24:49Z"},{"id":1819,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"SIGNAL-SPECIFIC TESTING:
- Bridge availability check: test graceful failure when bridge is down
- Bridge version compatibility: verify minimum signal-cli version
- Phone number format validation: E.164 format enforcement
- Group message handling: test group send/receive
- Attachment size limits: test file upload constraints
- Trust/safety key verification: test identity trust flow
- Polling interval: test configurable poll frequency
- Reconnection: test bridge restart recovery","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1","title":"Signal: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix","description":"Define the V3 contract for Signal before implementation expands. Pin down the auth boundary, configuration schema, operation inventory,  safety/risk/idempotency classifications, explicit non-goals, and how this connector maps onto the campaign-wide V3 acceptance contract. Define the supported  bridge topology, account-registration assumptions, daemon health requirements, and which messaging, group, attachment, and profile operations are in scope versus deferred.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:46:58.209777Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:29.507523Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:29.507136Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:46:58.209777Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:48:06.673286Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:48:20.224717Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1833,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for Signal (signal-cli bridge):

**Archetype**: Bridge/Daemon + Bidirectional (unique: requires external bridge process)
**Auth model**: signal-cli daemon must be running with registered phone number. Connector communicates via signal-cli JSON-RPC HTTP API (localhost). No direct Signal Protocol implementation — all crypto delegated to signal-cli.
**Network**: localhost only (bridge HTTP API). deny_private=true, deny_localhost=false.

**Bridge prerequisites**:
- signal-cli >= 0.13.0 installed and in PATH
- Phone number registered: signal-cli -u +NUMBER register / verify
- Daemon mode: signal-cli -u +NUMBER daemon --http --receive-mode=on-connection
- Bridge health endpoint: GET http://localhost:8080/v1/about

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| signal.send_message | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | signal.send |
| signal.send_reaction | Risky | Low | BestEffort | signal.send |
| signal.receive | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.list_contacts | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.list_groups | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.get_group | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.update_profile | Risky | Low | BestEffort | signal.admin |
| signal.delete_message | Dangerous | High | Strict | signal.send |
| signal.health | Safe | Low | Strict | signal.read |

**Safety policy**: End-to-end encryption handled by signal-cli. Connector never sees plaintext keys. Delete message is Dangerous (irreversible from sender perspective). Group creation/management deferred to future slice.

**Provisioning**: Manual phone registration via signal-cli CLI. Connector's doctor checks bridge availability, version, and registration status.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No group creation/administration, no file attachments > 100MB, no Signal Desktop linking, no direct libsignal integration.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8","title":"[T1-MSG.4] fcp.matrix — Matrix Federated Messaging Connector","description":"# Matrix Federated Messaging Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Matrix is the highest-value open and federated messaging addition in the campaign. It complements proprietary chat platforms by giving FCP a protocol-native path into self-hosted and federation-aware deployments.

## API surface and workflow role
The connector targets the Matrix Client-Server API and therefore needs to handle both read-heavy synchronization and write-capable room workflows:
- room and membership discovery,
- message and media send flows,
- sync-loop event ingestion,
- room state and membership transitions,
- configurable homeserver deployment assumptions.

## Expected operations
- `matrix.messages.send|get|list`
- `matrix.media.send|get`
- `matrix.rooms.list|get|join|leave`
- `matrix.members.list|invite`
- `matrix.sync.run`
- `matrix.rooms.set_topic`

## V3 implementation shape
This feature is intentionally planned as a five-step ladder:
1. define homeserver assumptions, auth boundary, `OperationInfo` safety semantics, and first-slice E2EE non-goals;
2. build typed client/config/error/state foundations;
3. wire runtime, manifest, operation handlers, and truthful introspection;
4. implement sync-loop state, membership, media, and event translation flows;
5. finish with readiness, federation-aware remediation guidance, observability, and conformance evidence.

## Critical considerations
- The first slice must be explicit about what is not yet supported around E2EE.
- Self-hosted homeserver configuration and federation caveats must appear in readiness and manifest surfaces.
- The sync loop is a core behavioral surface, not a side detail.
- Verification must show both safe read flows and mutation-heavy room workflows behave predictably under V3 semantics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:15.820830Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:37:09.733755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:15.820830Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1785,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:24:50Z"}]}
